reported_issue.symptom: Qwen/Qwen-coder XML tool call can arrive at clients as arguments: {} when the model emits a text preamble followed by <tool_call><function=exec_command></function></tool_call>
reported_issue.secondary_symptom: Responses raw SSE can violate output_index ordering when reasoning/message and function_call reuse output_index 0
reported_issue.assessment_boundary: The pasted root-cause assessment was not assumed; current source and focused tests were inspected and rerun.

current_source_contract.empty_required_args: vmlx_engine/server.py drops parsed tool calls missing required schema arguments after parser extraction and strips tool markup instead of emitting executable {} arguments.
current_source_contract.responses_streaming: Responses streaming parses separated content/reasoning/full-text candidates, prefers calls with non-empty arguments, and fail-closes required empty XML calls.
current_source_contract.output_index: Responses streaming reserves message output_index 0, reasoning output_index 1 when present, and advances function_call output_index after message/reasoning items.

proven: required empty XML function tags do not emit executable tool calls in current source
preamble plus empty XML tool call is rejected/fail-closed in streaming Responses
auto empty XML tool-call final markup is stripped rather than leaked
nonstream parser boundary drops empty required calls and strips markup
streaming Chat path does not emit executable empty-argument calls for preamble empty XML
reasoning-enabled Responses tool calls keep arguments
raw SSE classifier catches function_call reuse of message output_index
still_open: same-model live direct/gateway/tunnel raw SSE capture for the deployed reported alias
installed app UI proof with CHAT_DIAG request_shape and visible tool loop behavior
Qwen3.6 35B and qwen-coder-next live recurrence checks under Auto/on/off reasoning
public tunnel alias availability and exact routing
other_agent_next: Do not add a fake fallback that fabricates missing cmd arguments.
If a live model emits <function=exec_command></function>, expected current-source behavior is tool_calls_required failure or stripped markup, not arguments={}.
Capture raw SSE for the exact same served model through direct, gateway, and tunnel; compare output_item.added/done indices and function_call_arguments delta/done.
If deployed tunnel still emits {}, first verify it is running current server.py and parser files.
boundaries: no source code changed in this proof pass
